notes: Christian deaconess known for her austere and inflexible character, associated with John Chrysostom and imperial conflicts.

docs.title: Letter 50001
docs.author: John Chrysostom
docs.subject_summary: Please help support the mission of New Advent and get the full contents of this website as an instant download. Includes the Catholic Encyclopedia, Church Fathers, Summa, Bible and more — all for only
docs.excerpt: She herself however practised the most austere asceticism, renouncing the luxury of the bath, wearing none but old coarse clothing, and subjecting herself to severe restrictions in respect of food and sleep.

docs.title: Letter 3
docs.author: John Chrysostom
docs.subject_summary: Do not be anxious on my behalf, nor rack yourself with solicitude, on account of the severity of the winter, and the weakness of my digestion, and the incursions of the Isaurians. For the winter is on

docs.title: Letter 4
docs.author: John Chrysostom
docs.subject_summary: Having risen from the very gates of death I address this letter to the discreet lady; and I am very glad that your servants have met me just as I am anchoring at last in harbour. For had they met me w
docs.excerpt: For not only to bear misfortunes bravely but to be actually insensible to them, to overlook them, and with such little exertion to wreathe your brows with the garland prize of patience, neither labouring, nor
